claim: Agent benchmarks should evaluate not just the final answer but the full trajectory—every intermediate step, tool call, and output—to understand where agents lose points.
actionable: Collect the complete trajectory of your agent's execution and score each step against weighted criteria, not just the final result.
source_title: LLM Agent Eval with Trajectory Tracing — Rubricon

claim: YAML-defined rubrics with numeric scores and written rationales per criterion enable transparent and repeatable agent evaluation.
actionable: Define your evaluation criteria in YAML rubric files with weights, then pass full agent traces to an LLM judge that scores each criterion with both a rating and written justification.
source_title: LLM Agent Eval with Trajectory Tracing — Rubricon

claim: Rubricon is a tool that runs agent scenarios, collects trajectories, and compares scores across runs and model versions in a local web dashboard.
actionable: Use Rubricon (github.com/RitikPatill/rubricon) to define rubric YAML files, run agent tests, and view trajectory-level scoring comparisons in the local dashboard.
source_title: LLM Agent Eval with Trajectory Tracing — Rubricon
